title: India-US Trade Deal: First Tranche Days Away from Signing
summary: Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal declared all major points settled on the first tranche of a landmark India-US bilateral trade deal. Formal talks continue June 2–4, with India committed to purchasing $500 billion in US goods over five years.

coverages.headline: India-US Trade Pact to Signal US Tariff Modifications: Piyush Goyal
coverages.excerpt: Commerce Minister Goyal confirms all major points settled; officials from both sides continue talks June 2–4 to finalise the first tranche.
coverages.bias_notes: Frames the deal as a clear win with no caveats. Uses Goyal's statement directly without seeking opposition or independent economic analysis. Headline emphasises India's agency ('to signal') in a positive framing.

coverages.headline: India-US Trade Deal to Reflect Washington's Tariff Changes
coverages.excerpt: Piyush Goyal says the imminent deal will formally embed US tariff modifications, with India committed to purchasing $500B in US goods over five years.
coverages.bias_notes: More factual framing — note the headline leads with what India must concede ('reflect Washington's tariff changes') rather than what India gains. A subtle but meaningful difference in emphasis from Republic World's version.

coverages.headline: All Major Points Settled on India-US Trade Deal, Officials to Meet June 2–4
coverages.excerpt: The first tranche of a historic bilateral deal announced in February by Modi and Trump is now days away from formal signing, covering tariffs, market access, and investment norms.
coverages.bias_notes: Closest to wire-service neutrality. Attributes the claim to officials without amplifying it. Includes the February origin point — giving readers historical context rather than treating this as a sudden breakthrough.
